mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate near the zero lower bound reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically supporting valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2016-10-01, near post-crisis lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return hit rate in low-rate periods is not significantly above the unconditional 65.5% base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days once the threshold is crossed.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of 2016-10-01, near cycle lows
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, forward 20d returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly forecasts sustained earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; neither series alone captures this compounding demand signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage_value=465914 as of 2016-10-01, HOUST vintage_value=1047 as of 2016-09-01
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform single-series trigger episodes by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the joint signal is not higher than the max of the two individual series' Rank ICs over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During an expansionary macro regime (rising GDPC1 and low UNRATE), positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) confirms sustained real output expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; the same INDPRO signal is muted or reversed in contractionary regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE 4.9% and GDPC1 revised_value 19197.938 indicate ongoing expansion as of 2016-11
failure_condition: INDPRO-return Rank IC in expansion regimes is not statistically distinguishable from INDPRO-return Rank IC in contraction regimes (difference not significant at p<0.05), invalidating the regime-conditionality claim.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a always-on leading indicator of consumer income and spending capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in labor market strength.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage_value=144952 as of 2016-10-01 showing continued monthly growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips across more than two non-overlapping 3-year subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates above 2.5%, markets anticipate tighter future monetary policy from the Federal Reserve, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter once the threshold is breached.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value=241.176 as of 2016-09-01 with YoY growth approaching target
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following CPI threshold breaches are not significantly negative relative to unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or positive-return frequency in triggered episodes exceeds 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) during a late-cycle expansion (proxied by UNRATE at multi-year lows) signals an impending peak in interest-rate-sensitive demand and foreshadows a broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; the same HOUST decline in early-cycle regimes carries no predictive power.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% over trailing 6 months while UNRATE below 5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of 2016-10-01 consistent with late-cycle low unemployment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST-decline episodes during low-UNRATE regimes do not show significantly more negative 60d forward returns than HOUST-decline episodes during high-UNRATE regimes (difference not significant at p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production (INDPRO) and stagnant or falling payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates productivity-driven output gains without labor market confirmation, an unstable combination that precedes short-term equity market pullbacks over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices growth durability.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 0.3% AND PAYEMS MoM growth < 0.05%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=104.226 and PAYEMS vintage_value=144952 as of late 2016 showing diverging growth rates
failure_condition: Divergence episodes do not produce significantly more negative 5d forward returns than non-divergence periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction signal's Rank IC is not higher than either single-series Rank IC over any 2-year window.
